root_cause: MCP tools are registered in the ToolRegistry under namespaced names (e.g. 'mcp.weather.weather') but LLMs call them using the bare tool name from the MCP server's own definition (e.g. 'weather'). The repair_tool_name() function in tool_execution.rs only did exact + case-insensitive matching, so 'weather' was never repaired to 'mcp.weather.weather'. The pre-validation step then returned 'Tool not found', which was fed back to the LLM as an error — causing infinite retries without ever getting the real result. Secondary fix: ToolRegistry::execute() now normalises result.call_id to match tool_call.id so TUI sub-agent matching and trajectory logging always see the correct call ID regardless of what the Tool implementation returns (MCP bridge tools were fabricating 'mcp-{server}-{tool}' IDs).

changes.crates/ava-agent/src/agent_loop/tool_exe: Added MCP namespace repair in repair_tool_name(): when the LLM calls 'weather' and the registry has 'mcp.weather.weather', match the last dot-segment of registered tool names against the requested name (case-insensitive). First suffix match wins.
changes.crates/ava-tools/src/registry.rs: In ToolRegistry::execute(), after unwrapping the tool result, set result.call_id = tool_call.id.clone() to ensure the returned ToolResult always carries the actual LLM-assigned call ID.

test_output: Model successfully calls mcp.weather.weather for Tokyo and responds 'Sunny, 72F in Tokyo' without retrying
